<!-- # Security Allocation -->

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

function securityAllocationTable() {
  const data = equity_tickers ?? [];
  if (data.length === 0)
    return html`<div>No ticker data available</div>`;

  // Get ticker prices from processed quotes data
  const tickerPrices = processedQuotesData ?? {};

  /* ─── Totals by asset class ───────────────────────────── */
  let equityTotal = 0, bondTotal = 0;
  data.forEach(d => {
    if (d.Type === "Equity")  equityTotal += d.Risky_Pf_Weight;
    else if (d.Type === "Bond") bondTotal += d.Risky_Pf_Weight;
  });

  /* ─── Largest holding (optional row highlight) ────────── */
  const maxRisky = Math.max(...data.map(d => d.Risky_Pf_Weight));

  /* ─── HTML table ──────────────────────────────────────── */
  return html`
    <div style="overflow-y: auto;">
      <h5 class="table-title">Security Allocation</h5>
      <table class="table table-striped table-sm">
        <thead>
          <tr>
            <th>Asset Class</th>
            <th>Ticker</th>
            <th>Name</th>
            <th>Sector</th>
            <th>Asset Class&nbsp;Weight</th>
            <th>Risky Pf&nbsp;Weight</th>
            <th>Complete Pf&nbsp;Weight</th>
            <th>Last Price</th>
            <th>Shares</th>
            <th>Amount</th>
          </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          ${data
            .slice()                                            // keep source intact
            .sort((a, b) => b.Risky_Pf_Weight - a.Risky_Pf_Weight)
            .slice(0, 10)
            .map(d => {
              const classTotal     = d.Type === "Equity" ? equityTotal : bondTotal;
              const classWeight    = classTotal ? d.Risky_Pf_Weight / classTotal : 0;
              const riskyPfWeight  = classWeight * (d.Type === "Equity" ? equity_weight : bond_weight);
              const completeWeight = riskyPfWeight * fixed_optimal_weight;
              
              // Get the last price for this ticker
              const lastPrice = tickerPrices[d.Ticker]?.lastPrice;

              // Calculate amount and shares
              const amount = investment_amount * completeWeight;
              const shares = lastPrice ? amount / lastPrice : 0;

              return html`
                <tr>
                  <td>${d.Type}</td>
                  <td><strong>${d.Ticker}</strong></td>
                  <td>${d.Name}</td>
                  <td>${d.Sector}</td>
                  <td>${formatPercent(classWeight)}</td>
                  <td>${formatPercent(riskyPfWeight)}</td>
                  <td>${formatPercent(completeWeight)}</td>
                  <td>${lastPrice !== undefined ? formatCurrency(lastPrice, "$", 2) : "N/A"}</td>
                  <td>${shares > 0 ? formatNumber(shares, 2) : "N/A"}</td>
                  <td>${formatCurrency(amount)}</td>
                </tr>
              `;
            })}
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </div>`;
}
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Security Distribution

```{ojs}
viewof equityTickersBarChart = {
  // Grab the source array and short‑circuit if it’s empty
  const data = equity_tickers ?? [];
  if (data.length === 0) return html`<div>No ticker data available</div>`;

  // Totals by asset class
  let equityTotal = 0, bondTotal = 0;
  data.forEach(d => {
    if (d.Type === "Equity")      equityTotal += d.Risky_Pf_Weight;
    else if (d.Type === "Bond")   bondTotal  += d.Risky_Pf_Weight;
  });

  // Top‑10 tickers, converted to complete‑portfolio weights
  const barData = data
    .slice()                                       // don’t mutate the source
    .sort((a, b) => b.Risky_Pf_Weight - a.Risky_Pf_Weight)
    .slice(0, 10)
    .map(d => {
      const classTotal      = d.Type === "Equity" ? equityTotal : bondTotal;
      const classWeight     = classTotal > 0 ? d.Risky_Pf_Weight / classTotal : 0;
      const riskyPfWeight   = classWeight * (d.Type === "Equity" ? equity_weight : bond_weight);
      const completeWeight  = riskyPfWeight * fixed_optimal_weight;
      return {
        asset:  d.Ticker,          // use the same “asset” key as the other chart
        weight: completeWeight * 100   // %
      };
    });

  // Bar chart
  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 60,
    x: {
      label: "",
      domain: barData.map(d => d.asset),
      padding: 0.3
    },
    y: {
      label: "Weight (%)",
      domain: [0, Math.max(...barData.map(d => d.weight)) * 1.1],
      grid: true,
      tickFormat: d => d + "%"
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(barData, {
        x: "asset",
        y: "weight",
        fill: "asset",
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.asset}: ${d.weight.toFixed(1)}%`
      }),
      Plot.text(barData, {
        x: "asset",
        y: "weight",
        text: d => `${d.weight.toFixed(1)}%`,
        dy: -10,
        fontWeight: "bold",
        fontSize: 16
      })
    ],
    color: { legend: false }
  });
}
```

### Allocation Table

```{ojs}
securityAllocationTable()
```

<!-- 
## Equity Securities

::: {.panel-tabset style="height: calc(100vh - 450px); overflow-y: auto;"} -->

## Equity Efficient Frontier 

:::: {.rows}

### Plotly Interactive Chart {height=70%}

```{ojs}
html`
  <div class="card">
    <iframe 
      src="https://raw.githack.com/renan-peres/mfin-portfolio-management/refs/heads/main/reports/efficient_frontier_2025-04-26.html"
      frameborder="0" allow="clipboard-write" allowfullscreen>
    </iframe>
  </div>
`;
```

### Equities Weighted Retun {height=30%}

```{python}
# Order Columns
ef_desired_order = ['Ticker', 'Weight', 'Expected Return', 'Standard Deviation', 'Sharpe Ratio']
optimal_portfolio_ef = equity_disp.copy()  # Don't drop those columns
all_columns = optimal_portfolio_ef.columns.tolist()
first_columns = [col for col in ef_desired_order if col in all_columns]
optimal_portfolio_ef = optimal_portfolio_ef[first_columns].sort_values(by="Weight", ascending=False).transpose()

# Display the result
display(optimal_portfolio_ef)
```

:::

## Bond Securities

## Covariances
